question: Will Pablo Lopez win the 2026 AL Cy Young Award?
description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 American League Cy Young Award.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLB rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Baseball; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tarik Skubal's frontrunner status at 25% implied probability reflects his dominant 2024 AL Cy Young win (18-4, 2.39 ERA, 228 strikeouts), youth at 29 entering 2026, and long-term Tigers extension amid Detroit's rising contention. Dylan Cease edges others with elite strikeout rates (11.4 K/9) and free agency after 2025, fueling AL landing speculation. Jacob deGrom (11.5%) and Garrett Crochet (11%) hinge on health and innings durability post-injury/breakout years, while Mariners arms like George Kirby, Bryan Woo, and Logan Gilbert cluster due to rotation depth and sub-3.50 ERAs. Max Fried's 9.6% nods FA mobility to AL suitors, but wide dispersion underscores uncertainties in trades, workloads, and prospect maturation for a truly open field.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Paul Skenes as a slim 2026 NL Cy Young favorite at 27.5%, but Hunter Greene, Mitch Keller, and Jacob Misiorowski trail within 7 points, underscoring a crowded field of high-upside starters. Skenes' 2024 rookie dominance (1.96 ERA, 170 strikeouts) and Greene's triple-digit fastball fuel optimism for breakout campaigns, while Keller's Pirates rotation stability adds consistency. Brewers prospect Misiorowski's raw stuff (100+ mph heat, plus slider) injects volatility as a potential rookie phenom. Veterans like Chris Sale and Zack Wheeler lag due to injury histories and age, highlighting how health, innings durability, and team playoff pushes will decide this wide-open race over two seasons.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLB rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Baseball; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Paul Skenes as a slim 2026 NL Cy Young favorite at 27.5%, but Hunter Greene, Mitch Keller, and Jacob Misiorowski trail within 7 points, underscoring a crowded field of high-upside starters. Skenes' 2024 rookie dominance (1.96 ERA, 170 strikeouts) and Greene's triple-digit fastball fuel optimism for breakout campaigns, while Keller's Pirates rotation stability adds consistency. Brewers prospect Misiorowski's raw stuff (100+ mph heat, plus slider) injects volatility as a potential rookie phenom. Veterans like Chris Sale and Zack Wheeler lag due to injury histories and age, highlighting how health, innings durability, and team playoff pushes will decide this wide-open race over two seasons.

question: Foreign intervention in Gaza by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if police officers, security forces, or military personnel belonging to neither Israel nor a Palestinian entity begin an officially acknowledged police, military, peacekeeping, and/or security operation on the ground within Gaza by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The foreign personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of the Gaza Strip to qualify. Entering the maritime territory, as occurred during the US’s attempt to construct a floating pier in 2024, or entering the airspace will not qualify. 

Entering Israeli controlled buffer zones will not qualify.  

Egyptian or other forces operating solely along the Egyptian/Gaza border will not qualify regardless of if they cross the border. 

Foreign personnel carrying out individual special operations, diplomatic missions, or solely humanitarian operations will not qualify.

Qualifying deployments must involve active-duty personnel of at least one foreign state or international institution (e.g., the UN or multinational missions) who are officially sent to Gaza for an operational role (e.g., peacekeeping, convoy escort, detention, logistical support). To qualify, the deployment must occur within the specified timeframe; mere announcements will not suffice.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's market for foreign intervention in Gaza reflects low implied probabilities for direct military involvement by major powers like the US, Iran, or Arab states, driven by ongoing Israel-Hamas ceasefire talks mediated by Qatar, Egypt, and the US. Recent escalations include Hezbollah rocket exchanges with Israel along the Lebanon border and Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping, but no confirmed ground troop deployments beyond Israeli forces. Diplomatic pressure from the Biden administration, including arms shipment pauses, has tempered US intervention odds, while Iran's April missile barrage prompted restrained Israeli responses to avoid wider war. Upcoming UN Security Council votes and potential Netanyahu-Trump meetings post-US election could shift dynamics, underscoring inherent uncertainties in regional proxy conflicts.

question: Will "How to Make a Killing" score at least 56 on the Rotten Tomatoes Tomatometer?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the displayed Rotten Tomatoes “All Critics” Tomatometer score for How to Make a Killing (2026) is at least equal to the specified number at 10:00 AM ET on February 23, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If, for any reason, the resolution data is unavailable at this market's specified end time, the resolution source will be checked until the relevant data is available. This market will resolve to “No” if no data is available by February 27, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly backs a final Rotten Tomatoes Tomatometer score of 57% or higher for "How to Make a Killing," fueled by its current 57% rating from 14 early critic reviews following festival screenings. This indie comedy-thriller, starring Edward James Olmos and directed by Khalid Ferguson, has elicited mixed responses—praise for its quirky premise and performances amid gripes over pacing and originality—stabilizing the score amid low review volume. Historical precedents show early festival tallies often hold for smaller releases, supporting the near-certain 57+ positioning. An upset below 57% could stem from a pre-wide-release surge of scathing mainstream critiques, potentially dragging it into rotten territory if volume spikes unfavorably.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly backs a final Rotten Tomatoes Tomatometer score of 57% or higher for "How to Make a Killing," fueled by its current 57% rating from 14 early critic reviews following festival screenings. This indie comedy-thriller, starring Edward James Olmos and directed by Khalid Ferguson, has elicited mixed responses—praise for its quirky premise and performances amid gripes over pacing and originality—stabilizing the score amid low review volume. Historical precedents show early festival tallies often hold for smaller releases, supporting the near-certain 57+ positioning. An upset below 57% could stem from a pre-wide-release surge of scathing mainstream critiques, potentially dragging it into rotten territory if volume spikes unfavorably.

question: Will the Democratic Party win the popular vote in the 2026 U.S. House of Representatives midterm elections by 16% or more?
description:
This market will resolve according to the popular vote margin of victory between the Democratic Party and the Republican Party in the US House of Representatives midterm elections, scheduled for November 3, 2026.

For the purpose of this market, the “popular vote margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentage of valid votes cast for all Democratic Party House candidates and the percentage of valid votes cast for all Republican Party House candidates in this election. The percentage of the valid votes cast for each party’s House candidates will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes that party’s House candidates receive by the sum of all valid votes cast for U.S. House candidates in the election. Only votes cast for candidates for U.S. Representative (voting members of the House of Representatives from districts in the 50 states) will be considered; votes for Delegates and the Resident Commissioner will not be considered.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

This market will resolve based on the vote totals as published by the US Clerk of the House. Any recount included in the Clerk of the House’s publication will be considered.

If the US House of Representatives midterm elections do not take place by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the 2026 midterm election results published by the US Clerk of the House (https://history.house.gov/Institution/Election-Statistics/). If the 2026 midterm elections take place, and the Clerk of the House does not publish the results of this election by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source may be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a narrow Democratic House popular vote edge as most likely in 2026 midterms, with 4-10% leads at 23-25% each and "Other" outcomes—including tighter margins or Republican advantages—at 45.5%, reflecting historical midterm headwinds for the president's party averaging 3-5 point popular vote deficits. Recent generic ballot averages on RealClearPolitics show Democrats up 2.1 points nationally as of early December 2024, buoyed by post-election polling rebounds amid GOP's slim 220-215 House majority. District gerrymandering provides Republicans some buffer against seat losses despite popular vote gaps, while Democratic fundraising momentum and potential voter backlash on issues like inflation sustain trader focus on modest opposition gains. Early 2025 retirements and special election results could shift these dynamics.

question: Will Aaron Judge win the 2026 American League Hank Aaron Award?
description:
This market will resolve to the player who wins the 2026 American League Hank Aaron Award.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLB rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Major League Baseball; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bobby Witt Jr.'s breakout 2024 season, leading MLB with a .332 average and 32 homers, anchors trader consensus at 44.5% for the 2026 AL Hank Aaron Award, but Corey Seager's consistent .500-plus slugging despite injuries and José Ramírez's durable 39-homer power keep them clustered above 39%, reflecting uncertainty in projecting peak performance two years out. Emerging stars like Gunnar Henderson and Julio Rodríguez, both 24 with 30-homer potential, add volatility, while Aaron Judge's elite OPS+ faces strikeout scrutiny and Cal Raleigh's catcher production surprises at 35%. Youthful upside battles veteran reliability amid park factors and health risks, driving the wisdom of crowds to price a wide-open race.

question: Will James Talarico and Ken Paxton be the candidates for the Texas Senate Election?
description:
The 2026 United States Senate election in Texas is scheduled for November 3, 2026, to elect one member of the United States Senate to represent Texas. Party primary elections will be held on March 3, 2026, with runoff elections on May 26, 2026, if no candidate receives a majority in the primary.

This market will resolve based on the candidates who win the 2026 Democratic and Republican primary elections for United States Senator from Texas, including any required runoff elections.

This market may resolve as soon as the results of both parties' primaries are known definitively. If, for any reason, a candidate other than the primary election winner later becomes the official nominee, this will not affect this market’s resolution.

If a different combination of candidates than those listed wins the respective primaries, or if the results of the election aren’t known by November 30, 2026 ET, this market will resolve to “Other.”  

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Texas Republican Party and the Texas Democratic Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Texas Senate matchup favors State Rep. James Talarico as the Democratic nominee paired with either incumbent Sen. John Cornyn or Attorney General Ken Paxton at 55.5% and 43.5%, reflecting uncertainty in the GOP primary for the 2026 race. Cornyn's establishment profile faces pressure from Paxton's Trump-aligned base, amplified by recent Paxton criticisms of Cornyn's bipartisan deals and strong fundraising post-impeachment acquittal. Talarico's rising profile among progressive Democrats, bolstered by statewide visibility on education policy, positions him ahead of Rep. Jasmine Crockett or others. No major polling exists yet, but Paxton's potential entry—hinted in recent interviews—has traders weighing primary upset risks against Cornyn's incumbency advantages and donor network. Upcoming filing deadlines could shift odds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus for the 2026 NL Hank Aaron Award—honoring the league's top offensive performer—shows a razor-thin race among proven sluggers, with Ohtani, Harper, Betts, Acuña, and Tatis Jr. clustered within eight points, underscoring the National League's offensive depth. eventMetadata.context_description: The Los Angeles Dodgers dominate trader consensus at 43.5% implied probability for the 2026 NL pennant, fueled by their loaded rotation headlined by Yoshinobu Yamamoto and Tyler Glasnow, plus locked-in stars like Shohei Ohtani and Mookie Betts through long-term deals, building on their 2024 World Series title and perennial October prowess. The New York Mets surged to 11.5% following Juan Soto's blockbuster 15-year, $765 million signing on December 9, pairing him with Francisco Lindor for elite offense amid Steve Cohen's aggressive spending. Philadelphia Phillies (10%) and Atlanta Braves (8%) hold firm on ace pitchers Zack Wheeler/Zac Gallen and young cores, while Chicago Cubs (8%) gain traction from Craig Counsell's management and Tucker Heyward extensions, reflecting offseason roster upgrades shifting long-term NL power dynamics.

This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2026 MLB American League Central division.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official winner as determined by MLB rules. If multiple winners are announced then this market will resolve to the team whose listed nickname comes first alphabetically.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to be named the 2026 American League Central division champion per the rules of the MLB (e.g., they are eliminated in the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Major League Baseball (https://www.mlb.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Detroit Tigers lead trader consensus at 47.5% implied probability for the 2026 AL Central crown, fueled by their breakout 2024 playoff run and a burgeoning young core anchored by Cy Young winner Tarik Skubal, plus prospects like Jackson Jobe reaching prime form. Kansas City Royals trail at 28.5%, buoyed by Bobby Witt Jr.'s megadeal and elite bullpen continuity after their own Wild Card surge, though rotation depth remains a question. Cleveland Guardians sit at 16.0% amid steady farm system output but recent starter losses, while Minnesota Twins (10.0%) grapple with injury-prone pitching and payroll constraints; Chicago White Sox languish at 6.2% in rebuild mode. Recent offseason prospect graduations and arbitration wins tilt momentum toward Detroit's sustained contention edge.

eventMetadata.context_description: Seattle Mariners command 50% trader consensus for the 2026 AL West crown, propelled by their league-best pitching depth—anchored by contract-controlled aces Luis Castillo, Logan Gilbert, George Kirby, and Bryce Miller—alongside Julio Rodríguez's superstar potential and a top-tier farm system stocked with near-ready hitters like Cole Young. Houston Astros (24%) draw support from playoff pedigree and extensions for Yordan Alvarez and Framber Valdez, though an aging core post-Jose Altuve's free agency looms. Texas Rangers (20%) leverage 2023 title experience and Jacob deGrom's return, tempered by injury histories. Oakland Athletics (8%) and Angels (5.7%) trail amid rebuilds, weak rotations, and dismal 2024 finishes below .500. Recent prospect graduations and Mariners' midseason 2024 trades for offense signal upward momentum.

eventMetadata.context_description: The razor-thin odds atop the 2026 NL East futures—Phillies at 34%, Mets at 33.5%, and Braves at 26.5%—reflect trader consensus on a brutal three-team logjam driven by elite rotations, star-studded lineups, and aggressive offseason maneuvers. New York's blockbuster Soto signing supercharges their offense alongside Lindor and Alonso, erasing Atlanta's prior edge, while Philadelphia's Wheeler-Nola-Suarez trio and playoff pedigree maintain frontrunner status despite aging position players. The Braves' youth movement with Acuña and Strider harbors rebound potential post-injuries, amplified by Miami and Washington's rebuilds funneling wins upward. Recent momentum from 2024's Phillies division crown and Mets' NLCS run keeps probabilities bunched, underscoring division parity where pitching health and midseason trades could swing outcomes.

eventMetadata.context_description: The razor-thin odds atop the 2026 NL East futures—Phillies at 34%, Mets at 33.5%, and Braves at 26.5%—reflect trader consensus on a brutal three-team logjam driven by elite rotations, star-studded lineups, and aggressive offseason maneuvers. New York's blockbuster Soto signing supercharges their offense alongside Lindor and Alonso, erasing Atlanta's prior edge, while Philadelphia's Wheeler-Nola-Suarez trio and playoff pedigree maintain frontrunner status despite aging position players. The Braves' youth movement with Acuña and Strider harbors rebound potential post-injuries, amplified by Miami and Washington's rebuilds funneling wins upward. Recent momentum from 2024's Phillies division crown and Mets' NLCS run keeps probabilities bunched, underscoring division parity where pitching health and midseason trades could swing outcomes.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Los Angeles Dodgers' overwhelming 85.5% implied probability as 2026 NL West favorites stems from their unmatched roster depth, anchored by Shohei Ohtani's dual-threat dominance, Mookie Betts' consistency, and a rotation bolstered by Yoshinobu Yamamoto and Tyler Glasnow, all secured through massive long-term commitments following their 2024 World Series triumph. Their sustained payroll flexibility and farm system talent edge out rivals, reflected in trader consensus pricing. However, realistic challengers include catastrophic injuries to Ohtani or the core infield, San Diego Padres' potential rebound via Xander Bogaerts and Manny Machado staying healthy with pitching reinforcements, or Arizona Diamondbacks leveraging Corbin Carroll's speed and youth for a repeat of their 2023 surge amid any Dodger regression.

question: Will Russia capture Kostyantynivka by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the Kostyantynivka railroad station located on Pravoberezhna vulytsia by June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET.

The railroad station will be considered captured if any part of the train station icon is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of if it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the railroad station, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Train Station Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka3.jpg

Train Station Location in Kostyantynivka: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka2.jpg

Kostyantynivka Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Kostyantynivka1.jpg

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/hZ6ch5jVRvkNFY317

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map which will not be considered.

events.title: Will Russia capture Kostyantynivka by...?
events.description: This is a market on the likelihood of Russia capturing Kostyantynivka by a specific date.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have made incremental advances in Donetsk Oblast, capturing villages like Myrolyubivka and Vyshneve southeast of Kostyantynivka, but the town remains under Ukrainian control amid intense fighting over key heights and supply routes. Trader sentiment on Polymarket leans against a near-term capture, with implied probabilities reflecting the wisdom of crowds betting on Ukrainian defenses bolstered by recent Western aid deliveries, despite manpower strains and drone warfare disrupting Russian logistics. Primary drivers include Pokrovsk direction momentum, where Kostyantynivka's strategic rail junctions could enable further encirclement if breached. Upcoming assaults or F-16 deployments could shift dynamics, though front-line stalemates persist per official military updates.

question: Will Russia capture Lyman by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the Lyman railroad station located on Vulytsya Pryvokzalʹna by June 30, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET.

The railroad station will be considered captured if any part of the train station icon is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

If Russia comes into control of this territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. An announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

Once Russia captures the railroad station, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

Train Station Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/lyman+train+station+zoom.png

Train Station Location in Lyman: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/lyman+train+station.jpeg

Lyman Location: https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/lyman.jpeg

Location on Google maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/zeVSXaeDH93mF2WPA

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ukraine holds Lyman in Donetsk Oblast, a key rail hub recaptured in late 2022, driving low trader consensus for Russian capture by year-end as no major assaults have materialized recently. Russian forces have advanced incrementally toward Pokrovsk, about 30km southeast, capturing villages like Shevchenko but facing fierce Ukrainian resistance bolstered by drone strikes and fortifications. Official Ukrainian reports detail repelled probes around Lyman, while Russian claims remain unverified. Winter weather looms as a potential brake on offensives, alongside uncertainties in Western aid continuity post-US election. Traders weigh slow Russian advance rates—historically under 20 sq km daily—against fortified defenses shaping slim implied probabilities.

question: Will Russia capture all of Kostyantynivka by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures the entirety of Kostyantynivka, Donetsk Oblast, (48.528896° N, 37.702514° E) by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET.

Kostyantynivka will be considered captured if the entirety of the municipality is shaded red on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. If the area is not shaded red by the resolution date, the market will resolve to “No”.

Any continuous shading which reflects either “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. “Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine” will not qualify.

The territory of the municipality is shaded in light grey and is distinguished from bordering municipalities with a darker grey line. If all area within the municipality is shaded in red, however, the shading does not precisely match up with the border such that there is a tiny amount of grey along the border of the city, this will still qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces continue incremental advances toward Kostyantynivka in Donetsk Oblast on the Pokrovsk axis but have not secured full control, anchoring trader consensus at modest implied probabilities for capture by the market's deadline. Key recent drivers include Russian claims of entering southern outskirts after taking nearby Vodiane in late October, per Defense Ministry statements, offset by Ukrainian military reports of holding central positions through drone strikes and fortifications amid manpower strains. Grinding attrition warfare persists, with Russian superiority in artillery and infantry testing defenses, while incoming winter conditions could slow momentum. Traders monitor daily ISW assessments and frontline footage for shifts, absent major scheduled events.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have intensified assaults toward Lyman in Ukraine's Donetsk region, capturing nearby villages like Novomykhailivka and pushing from the north, but Ukrainian defenses continue holding the town itself amid heavy fighting. Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects skepticism of a full capture by the deadline, pricing in Russia's slow attritional gains—bolstered by manpower rotations and artillery dominance—against Ukraine's fortified lines, ammunition constraints, and counterbattery fire. Recent Institute for the Study of War assessments note incremental Russian advances but no breakthrough, with odds swayed by stalled momentum. Upcoming factors include seasonal mud potentially halting offensives, delayed Western aid deliveries, and potential Ukrainian reinforcements, underscoring high uncertainty in frontline dynamics.

question: Purdue Fort Wayne Mastodons vs. Milwaukee Panthers (W)
description:
In the upcoming WBB game, scheduled for February 25 at 6:00 PM ET:

If the Purdue Fort Wayne Mastodons win, the market will resolve to "Purdue Fort Wayne Mastodons".

If the Milwaukee Panthers win, the market will resolve to "Milwaukee Panthers".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no criminal charges against Prince Andrew by March 31, driven by the absence of any active investigation from UK authorities like the Metropolitan Police or Crown Prosecution Service, which previously reviewed Epstein-related allegations and declined to pursue them. question: Prince Andrew sentenced to prison?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Prince Andrew is sentenced to any time in jail/prison for any charges, by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will remain open through December 31, 2026 or until Prince Andrew is sentenced to time in jail/prison, regardless of any initial sentencing that does not include prison. Any sentencing will qualify, regardless of subsequent appeals or changes to the sentence.

Civil lawsuits, regulatory actions, administrative complaints, investigations without charges, internal employment actions, or public accusations without the filing of criminal charges will not qualify, even if widely reported or described colloquially as “charges.”

If at any point all charges against Prince Andrew for all criminal cases are dropped for whatever reason, due to a plea agreement without an admission of guilt, a dismissal, or any other situation in which no judgement is rendered or will be rendered by the court, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the relevant government and law enforcement; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Prince Andrew sentenced to prison?

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Prince Andrew is sentenced to any time in jail/prison for any charges, by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market will remain open through December 31, 2026 or until Prince Andrew is sentenced to time in jail/prison, regardless of any initial sentencing that does not include prison. Any sentencing will qualify, regardless of subsequent appeals or changes to the sentence.

Civil lawsuits, regulatory actions, administrative complaints, investigations without charges, internal employment actions, or public accusations without the filing of criminal charges will not qualify, even if widely reported or described colloquially as “charges.”

If at any point all charges against Prince Andrew for all criminal cases are dropped for whatever reason, due to a plea agreement without an admission of guilt, a dismissal, or any other situation in which no judgement is rendered or will be rendered by the court, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus assigns an 87.5% implied probability to "No" on Prince Andrew receiving a prison sentence, driven by the complete absence of criminal charges or active prosecution. UK police closed their Epstein-linked inquiry in 2015 with no action, and the 2022 civil settlement with Virginia Giuffre—reportedly for £12 million—resolved her claims without admission of liability. Recent unsealed US court documents in January 2024 referenced past allegations against Andrew but yielded no FBI indictment or extradition moves after years of review. King Charles's decision to strip his military titles and public funding further isolates him socially, not legally, underscoring minimal pathway to trial amid expired statutes of limitations on key claims.

question: Will Prince Andrew record an interview by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor (formerly Prince Andrew) records an interview between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM GMT. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An interview is defined as a scheduled, recorded conversation where Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor answers questions posed by a reporter or host, or multiple reporters/hosts, in a format intended for public release. Television interviews/appearances, podcasts, or other forms of direct, scheduled, recorded interviews will count. Press conferences, media briefings, or other unscheduled public questionings (i.e. answering questions while transiting from court or jail) will not count. 

A credible consensus of reporting indicating that Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor has recorded a qualifying interview within this market’s timeframe will be sufficient for a “Yes” resolution, even if that interview is not yet released.

The resolution sources for this market will be publicly available video or audio of a qualifying interview and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 97.6% implied probability for Prince Andrew recording an interview by March 31, driven by the complete absence of any official announcements, scheduled appearances, or credible media reports indicating plans. Since his ill-received 2019 BBC Newsnight interview and the 2022 settlement in his sexual assault case, the Duke of York has withdrawn from public life, with Buckingham Palace stating he will not resume royal duties. No recent developments, such as outreach to journalists or promotional hints, have emerged despite the approaching deadline. Realistic shifts could involve a surprise media deal or family reversal, though historical caution and royal protocol make this improbable.

question: US forces in Venezuela again by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Venezuela at any point between market creation and the specified date, 11:59 PM ET.

Military special operation forces will qualify; however, intelligence operatives will not count. 

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Venezuela to qualify. Entering Venezuela's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Venezuela for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a very low probability of US forces entering Venezuela, driven by the absence of any official military signals despite post-election tensions after Nicolas Maduro's disputed July 28 victory claim. US responses have centered on sanctions against Maduro allies, recognition of opposition leader Edmundo Gonzalez's tally, and State Department calls for peaceful resolution, explicitly avoiding intervention amid domestic priorities like the Ukraine and Middle East conflicts. Recent Venezuelan crackdowns on protesters and Essequibo border rhetoric with Guyana heighten risks, but US policy emphasizes diplomacy. The November 5 presidential election looms as a potential catalyst for hawkish shifts under a new administration.

question: Will there be less than 85m votes cast in the 2026 U.S. House of Representatives midterm elections?
description:
The US House of Representatives midterm elections are scheduled to be held on November 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the total number of votes cast for U.S. House candidates in the 2026 US House of Representatives midterm elections.

Only votes cast for candidates for U.S. Representative (voting members of the House of Representatives from districts in the 50 states) will be considered; votes for Delegates and the Resident Commissioner will not be considered.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the US House of Representatives midterm elections do not take place by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the 2026 midterm election results published by the US Clerk of the House (https://history.house.gov/Institution/Election-Statistics/). If the 2026 midterm elections take place, and the Clerk of the House does not publish the results of this election by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source may be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on 2026 midterm House turnout centers around 110-125 million votes, mirroring historical benchmarks like 2022's 111 million and 2018's 118 million ballots cast amid competitive national environments. This tight clustering reflects uncertainty over post-2024 dynamics, including presidential approval ratings, economic indicators, and partisan mobilization efforts, with no dominant catalyst yet emerging to push toward extremes like sub-100 million or over-130 million. Odds remain balanced as traders weigh voter fatigue from the presidential cycle against potential enthusiasm from gerrymander challenges or key district races; separation could arise from 2025 voter registration surges, early generic ballot polls, or midterm-specific endorsements.

Only votes cast for candidates for U.S. Representative (voting members of the House of Representatives from districts in the 50 states) will be considered; votes for Delegates and the Resident Commissioner will not be considered.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the US House of Representatives midterm elections do not take place by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the 2026 midterm election results published by the US Clerk of the House (https://history.house.gov/Institution/Election-Statistics/). If the 2026 midterm elections take place, and the Clerk of the House does not publish the results of this election by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source may be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on 2026 midterm House turnout centers around 110-125 million votes, mirroring historical benchmarks like 2022's 111 million and 2018's 118 million ballots cast amid competitive national environments. This tight clustering reflects uncertainty over post-2024 dynamics, including presidential approval ratings, economic indicators, and partisan mobilization efforts, with no dominant catalyst yet emerging to push toward extremes like sub-100 million or over-130 million. Odds remain balanced as traders weigh voter fatigue from the presidential cycle against potential enthusiasm from gerrymander challenges or key district races; separation could arise from 2025 voter registration surges, early generic ballot polls, or midterm-specific endorsements.

question: UEFA Champions League: Unbeaten Champion
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the 2025-26 UEFA Champions League champion goes unbeaten in every match during the Knockout Stages of the 2025-26 UEFA Champions League competition. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purpose of this market, “unbeaten” is defined as having not recorded a loss during any match in any stage of the Knockout Stages. The “Knockout Stages” include every match starting with the Knockout Play-Offs carrying each round of matches through, and including, the Champions League Final.

If the 2025-26 Champions League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and a team is confirmed as unbeaten through the completed matches prior to cancellation or postponement, this market will resolve to “Yes”. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The revamped UEFA Champions League league phase, with each of 36 teams playing eight matches, has introduced unprecedented parity, as early upsets like Lille's win over Real Madrid and Juventus beating Manchester City have dented unbeaten records of top contenders. Currently, Liverpool (four wins) and Barcelona (three wins, one draw) lead unbeaten, but their remaining fixtures against elite sides like Bayern Munich and Inter Milan heighten slip-up risks amid fixture congestion and rotation. Trader consensus at 50.5% for "No" reflects this balance, with historical rarity—no unbeaten champion since AC Milan's 1991-92 run—tempering optimism. A dominant streak by Liverpool could boost "Yes," while further stumbles by frontrunners would solidify "No."

question: Will Harry Kane record the most Player of the Match awards during the 2025 UEFA Champions League?
description:
This market will resolve to the player who collects the most Player of the Match awards through all rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Champions League competition.
		
In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Champions League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Champions League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/news/029d-1ebd2e90ac06-6c331a7ee578-1000--every-uefa-champions-league-player-of-the-match/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Harry Kane's explosive start in the UEFA Champions League league phase, securing three Player of the Match awards across Bayern Munich's dominant wins—including a brace and assist in their latest 4-0 rout—has propelled him to a commanding 60.5% implied probability as the trader consensus favorite for most overall honors. Kylian Mbappé trails at 22.5% after two POTMs for Real Madrid, buoyed by his hat-trick heroics, while Victor Osimhen (15.2%) and Fermín López (14.5%) gained ground with standout single awards in Napoli's and Barcelona's upsets. Nuno Mendes (13.5%) impressed for PSG, but Virgil van Dijk's defensive masterclass (9.1%) underscores Liverpool's solidity. Recent Bayern fixtures favor Kane's goalscoring edge, with four more league-phase games amplifying his lead amid fluid trader sentiment ahead of Matchday 4.

question: Will Arsenal score the most goals in 2025-26 UEFA Champions League?
description:
This market will resolve to the club that records the most total goals through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Champions League competition.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Champions League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the club who advanced farther in the competition. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the club that is listed first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Champions League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/statistics/clubs/goals/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus tilts toward Tottenham's Guglielmo Vicario for most Champions League clean sheets, reflecting Spurs' defensive resurgence with two shutouts in four league-phase matches amid a compact backline under Ange Postecoglou. Vicario's edge over Real Madrid's Thibaut Courtois—who has one clean sheet post-injury recovery—stems from Tottenham's easier remaining fixtures and higher clean-sheet rate (50%), versus Madrid's concession-prone attack-first style despite Courtois' elite shot-stopping. In a wide-open race, differentiators include team progression odds for extra games, home splits, and rivals like Porto's Diogo Costa (three clean sheets) or Dortmund's Gregor Kobel, whose paths hinge on knockout survival and momentum.

question: Will Álvaro Fernández Carreras record the most cards in 2025-26 UEFA Champions League?
description:
This market will resolve to the player who records the most disciplinary cards through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Champions League competition.

For the purpose of this market, a disciplinary card will count as a yellow card, a direct red card, or a red card resulting from the accumulation of two yellow cards in the same match. A 2nd yellow card accumulated in a match which results in a red card will count as one card in the final count, not a separate yellow and red card. The tally of these cards will be counted as reflected on the official UEFA Champions League website (https://www.uefa.com/uefachampionsleague/statistics/players/disciplinary/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus clusters tightly around midfield enforcers Martín Zubimendi and Konrad Laimer at 45% implied probabilities, with Declan Rice and Álvaro Fernández Carreras close behind, reflecting their shared status as the UCL's most-booked players with three or four yellows each through the league phase. Zubimendi and Mikel Merino's aggressive tackling for Real Sociedad, Laimer and Kim Min-Jae's combative roles at Bayern Munich, Rice's box-to-box intensity for Arsenal, and Fernández Carreras' overlapping runs at Benfica have fueled frequent cautions amid high-stakes fixtures. With 10 league-phase games remaining for most, plus potential knockouts, congested schedules and rivalry pressures keep foul risks elevated, sustaining the bunched odds as no player pulls ahead decisively.
